TextENGNew: A crowd of classmates was playing freely in the schoolyard.
Some were just bursting out of the shoe lockers, tapping their toes into their shoes as they ran.
changeReason: Changes location from 'school building' to 'shoe lockers' (昇降口 = entrance/shoe area).

TextENGNew: ...Looked like they'd just finished eating lunch.@k ...From a timing perspective, I'd gotten it about right.
changeReason: Changes 'time-manipulating standpoint' to 'timing perspective'; old translation added unintended sci-fi connotation.

TextENGNew: ``Oh... Maebara-san!@k@vS03/00/girlA04002. Are you okay...?!``
A few girls spotted me and came running over.
...Well, of course they would.
If someone wasn't there in the morning, you'd naturally assume they were absent for the day.
``Well, I'm... just late, that's all.@k@vS03/01/140100291. Don't worry about it.@k@vS03/01/140100292. ...More importantly... how's Satoko?@k@vS03/01/140100293. ...Did she come today?``
The girls exchanged glances.
...Seeing the shadow that passed over their faces, I knew the answer without having to hear it.
...Satoko was absent again today.
After being out for three days, she'd come back for just one.
And now she was out again.@k ...There was no guarantee she'd show up tomorrow, either.
``...Yeah.@k@vS03/00/girlA04004. But the teacher said Satoko-chan called this morning.``
``She said it was a cold!``
``Chie-sensei told everyone to make sure they gargle when they get home!``
changeReason: Changes 'rinse their mouths' to 'gargle' (うがい = gargling).

TextENGNew: ...Come on, Sensei.
...Don't you think you're being a little too oblivious...?@k You're a teacher, aren't you?@k Can't you be more perceptive and pick up on the SOS your student is sending...?
But... even if she did catch on, there was nothing we could hope for.
...The most she could do was a home visit.
...Which would only anger Satoko's uncle... and deepen Satoko's suffering...
``...She's right.@k@vS03/01/140100295. Make sure you guys don't catch a cold, okay?@k@vS03/01/140100296. ...You can go now.@k@vS03/01/140100297. There's still plenty of lunch break left.``
The girls scattered excitedly back into the schoolyard.
``...Oh... Keiichi-kuuun!!!``
Rena was yelling and waving from the classroom window.
...A moment later, Mion and Rika-chan poked their heads out too.
``...We were so worried... that even you might be absent today, Keiichi.``
``Thank goodness!@k@vS03/02/140200088. Your color looks okay -- you seem fine, at least!``
...Being told outright that I didn't look sick rubbed me the wrong way, but I decided not to dwell on it.
``...What happened?@k@vS03/03/140300133. Oversleep?``
``...Late is late, right?@k@vS03/01/140100299. ...Or would she let it slide if I said I was late because of a train accident?``
``...You overslept?``
...Mion was being a bit persistent.
Was she putting on her class president hat and scolding me for being late?@k ...She probably didn't mean anything by it, so I just nodded and agreed that yes, I'd overslept.
changeReason: Changes 'club president' to 'class president' (委員長 refers to class representative).

TextENGNew: ``There's still bento left!@k@vS03/02/140200090. Let's all eat together!@k@vS03/02/140200091. It's just not the same without you, Keiichi-kun!``
``...Satoko's... still out, huh?``
...Even Rena, who had been putting on a brave face, couldn't hold it together at that. Her expression darkened.
``She called this morning, apparently.@k@vS03/02/140200094. ...Said her cold relapsed and she'd be out again.@k@vS03/02/140200095. She mentioned it might drag on for a while.``
``...She must be lonely, so we were all talking about paying her a visit.``
changeReason: Changes 'sad' to 'lonely' (かわいそう can mean either; different emotional implication).

TextENGNew: ``...What are you saying, Rika-chan?``
Rika-chan was smiling broadly, as though it were some happy occasion, and it irritated me enough to cut her off.
...Rika-chan furrowed her brow... hung her head... and murmured a quiet apology.
``...It's not just you, Keiichi-kun.@k@vS03/02/140200097. ...This is hard... for all of us...``
``I know I'm not the only one struggling.@k@vS03/01/140100306. But it's true that none of us can actually do anything, right?``
...I had a feeling this exchange would lead to another fight if it continued.@k So I apologized first.
``Sorry.@k@vS03/01/140100308. ...I take it back, so please forget what I just said.@k@vS03/01/140100309. ...It's hard for everyone, I know.@k@vS03/01/140100310. ...Yeah.``
But it was painfully clear that I was the only one who had agonized to this extent over how to save her.
...At the very least, the others had all gone to sleep at a normal time last night.@k Slept soundly, most likely.@k ...They certainly hadn't stayed up all night... thinking desperately...
changeReason: Changes 'good dreams' to 'slept soundly' (快眠 = good sleep, not dreams).

TextENGNew: ``...I know it might not be what you want to hear right after getting to school.@k@vS03/03/140300136. But you were late today, Kei-chan.@k@vS03/03/140300137. ...Did it have anything to do with Satoko's absence?``
``Huh...?!@k@vS03/01/140100312. Mion, what are you talking about...?``
``...You can be a little reckless sometimes, Kei-chan.
...When you were absent this morning along with Satoko...@k@vS03/03/140300140. We seriously thought you might have taken her from her house and hidden her somewhere.``
changeReason: Removes loaded term 'kidnapped' in favor of neutral 'taken from her house'; different connotation.

TextENGNew: ...Easy for her to say.
changeReason: Changes addressee from 'you' to 'her', altering who the narrator is addressing.

TextENGNew: If I could have done that, I'd have already done it last night.
...None of them had given any real thought... to just how impossible that simple-sounding solution actually was.
I had thought about it all last night. Thought about it until the sky began to lighten.
...And in the end... I concluded... that there was no way I could pull it off.
``...Don't be ridiculous.@k@vS03/01/140100314. ...You can't just... hide a person like that.``
``.........You're right.@k@vS03/03/140300142. ...I'm sorry.@k@vS03/03/140300143. I apologize if that upset you.``
``Don't worry about it.@k@vS03/01/140100316. ...Anyway, let's eat something.@k@vS03/01/140100317. I'm starving.``
I pulled my bento box out of my bag.
Everyone shifted to make room for me to sit.
``Maebara-kun...!@k@vS03/22/141400010. Did you just get here?``
I was about to dig in with my chopsticks when... it was Sensei.
``Oh... yes.@k@vS03/01/140100319. ...Sorry for being late.@k@vS03/01/140100320. I overslept.``
...What was that about?
Even the teacher... was giving me this complicated, searching look.
``When you've finished eating, could the class president and Maebara-kun come to the teachers' lounge?@k@vS03/22/141400013. ...There's something I'd like to discuss.``
...It was probably about Satoko.
Sensei left it at that and headed back into the hallway.
``...Lunch can wait.@k@vS03/01/140100322. ...Let's go, Mion.``
``Mm... right.``
Mion and I exchanged a look, nodded to each other, and got to our feet.
``...It's probably... about Satoko-chan.``
No doubt about it.
...The official story was that she had a cold, but the whole class had some sense of the truth.
...I couldn't imagine the teacher hadn't caught wind of it too.
``...I kept playing dumb when she asked... so she probably gave up on asking me.``
...Even if they told the teacher, it would only make things worse.@k ...Was that why Rika-chan had kept silent?
...Or was it because, as Satoko's closest friend, she knew... that Satoko believed her uncle's cruelty was a trial she had to endure... so that her brother would come home?
``...What should we do?@k@vS03/03/140300146. ...Maybe we should figure out where we all stand before we go in there.``
``...What do you want to do, Rika-chan?``

changeReason: Changes Rika's catchphrase rendering from 'Meep' to 'Mii' (みぃ).

TextENGNew: Rika-chan was Satoko's closest friend.
...No matter how much I played the role of her 'Nii-nii'... it was nothing compared to the time Rika-chan and Satoko had spent together.
...So I felt her opinion deserved the most weight.
``...I know why Satoko is trying so hard to endure this.``
Everyone started... and turned to face me.

``...Back then, whenever Satoko was going through something painful... she'd cry and hide behind her brother's back.@k@vS03/01/140100326. ...But now Satoko believes... that in the end... that's why her brother ran away.``
``......Who... told you that...?``
``The coach, I'll bet...``
I nodded quietly.
``But... that belief has nothing to do with whether Satoshi comes back.@k@vS03/01/140100329. ...I hate to say this about Satoko... but that's the reality.``
Everyone... hung their heads.
...Everyone... was thinking the same thing...
``...Satoko... probably won't welcome anyone's help.@k@vS03/01/140100331. ...She wants to endure it all on her own.@k@vS03/01/140100332. .........But is it... truly right... to respect that...?``

``...I don't think it is.@k@vS03/01/140100334. ...Even if Satoko resents us for a while... there are times when you have to save someone against their will.@k@vS03/01/140100335. ...And I believe... that time is now.``
...I told them everything: what I'd seen and felt when I went to Satoko's house yesterday.
``......Keiichi-kun... so you think... we should tell someone, right?``
Meanwhile... Rika-chan quietly raised her hand... as if asking for permission to speak.
``...I'll leave it... to Keiichi.``
``Huh... is that okay, Rika-chan...?``
``...Keiichi has been thinking about Satoko... more than anyone else here.@k@vS03/05/140500021. ...If Keiichi has decided... that it's time to speak up, then I have no objections.``
``...Thanks. ...Mion, Rena -- are you both okay with that?``
Mion seemed to hesitate, unsure whether to nod.
...But when she saw Rena give a firm nod, she steeled herself and nodded too.
``All right.@k@vS03/01/140100341. ...Let's go, Mion.``
Only Chie-sensei was in the teachers' lounge.
...The principal was nowhere to be seen.
...A glance at the schedule board showed 'Training -- Going straight home' next to the principal's name.
...The principal was a man with an overpowering sense of justice.
...Satoko's problem couldn't be solved with something as vague as a sense of justice.@k ...His absence might actually be fortunate.
When Sensei saw Mion and me come in, she covered her curry bento and set her meal aside.
``Please have a seat.``
She directed us to the guest chairs facing the principal's desk.
...I hadn't sat in these chairs since I came to handle my transfer paperwork.
The two of us sat there uncomfortably... before Sensei walked over with a notebook and ballpoint pen and sat across from us.
``Maebara-kun, you were absent this morning. Did something happen?``
``I'm sorry, I overslept.``
``...I have no reason to lie about it...``
Sensei narrowed her eyes as if pressing the point.
...Now I was certain.
...She wanted to talk about Satoko.
``...I believe you both already know, but your friend Houjou-san has been absent quite frequently of late.``
Neither Mion nor I responded.@k ...But Sensei continued, undeterred.
``...Do either of you know what's going on?@k@vS03/22/141400019. There are people in the class spreading troubling rumors.``
Mion kept her head down and stayed silent, as though she were being lectured.
...She sent me a quick sidelong glance.@k ...We'd all decided earlier.@k ...The signal that they were leaving this to me.
``Class president?@k@vS03/22/141400021. Maebara-kun?@k@vS03/22/141400022. ...I'm not angry, you know.@k@vS03/22/141400023. I'm simply asking you to tell me if you know something.``
changeReason: Changes form of address from 'Mion-kun' to 'Class president' (委員長).

TextENGNew: ``...Sensei.@k@vS03/01/140100345. May I ask you something first?``
She clearly hadn't expected me to turn it around on her.@k ...She looked taken aback.
``...I don't know what rumors are going around the class about Satoko.@k@vS03/01/140100347. ...But hypothetically, if those rumors were true... what would you do, Sensei?``
``W-well, what would I do?@k@vS03/22/CHIE040302. ...If it's true, I certainly can't ignore it.``
``'Can't ignore it' is vague. What exactly would you do?``
Sensei's eyebrows shot up.@k ...Perhaps I'd come across as combative.
``Maebara-kun!@k@vS03/22/141400027. I'm being completely serious here!!``

``...So am I, Sensei.@k@vS03/01/140100350. ...I am being deadly serious right now.@k@vS03/01/140100351. Please answer my question first.@k@vS03/01/140100352. ...If the rumors are true, what exactly would you do about it?``
Sensei... paused for a few breaths... then opened her mouth slowly.
``F-first...@k@vS03/22/141400029. I would visit Houjou-san's home to confirm the situation.``
``Her uncle might yell at you and turn you away.@k@vS03/01/140100354. ...But fine.@k@vS03/01/140100355. ...Let's say you manage to meet with Satoko and her uncle.@k@vS03/01/140100356. Then what?``
``I would get to the truth.@k@vS03/22/141400031. Once the facts are confirmed... I would take action.``
changeReason: Changes vague 'I'd go from there' to more definitive 'I would take action' (指導します = will provide guidance/take action).

TextENGNew: ``Please stop using vague terms like 'take action.'@k@vS03/01/140100358. What would you specifically do?``
``I would report it to the Okinomiya child welfare center.@k@vS03/22/CHIE040304. You said just now that her uncle might turn me away, didn't you?``
changeReason: Changes 'child consultation center' to 'child welfare center' (生活相談所).

TextENGNew: ``Child welfare officers have the authority to bring police along when the situation warrants it.@k@vS03/22/CHIE040306. They will not yield to intimidation.``
...I was supposed to get a guarantee that Satoko would be saved before telling her anything, yet here I was, essentially spilling everything from the start.
...Even though my tone had been this confrontational, Sensei held her emotions in check and listened calmly.
...She was a good teacher.
...Chie-sensei genuinely cared about her students.
...But... she didn't have the power or authority to save Satoko.
Sensei glanced at the clock, stood, and reached for the phone on the principal's desk.
``...Is this okay, Kei-chan?@k@vS03/03/140300153. ...Is this... really going to be all right...?``
It was too late now.@k ...The die had been cast.
...All we could do now... was pray.

``Oh, thank you for taking my call.@k@vS03/22/141400043. This is Chie, a teacher at the Hinamizawa branch school. Is Director Watanabe in the guidance office?@k@vS03/22/141400044. ...Yes, that's right.@k@vS03/22/141400045. ...Something urgent has come up, and I need to reach the principal as soon as possible.``
``...Yes.@k@vS03/22/141400047. Thank you.@k@vS03/22/141400048. Please tell him it's urgent.@k@vS03/22/141400049. I appreciate it...``
Sensei put down the phone...@k then tilted her head back and took a deep breath, the way I often did.
``...I'll ask you both one more time.@k@vS03/22/141400051. ...Are the rumors going around the class... really true?``
``.........Yes.@k@vS03/01/140100373. ...Yesterday, I went to Satoko's house... and saw for myself what's really going on.``
``Leave the rest to me.@k@vS03/22/141400053. I will do everything in my power for Satoko-chan.``
changeReason: Changes 'I'll definitely do something' to 'I will do everything in my power' (何とかします); new version expresses stronger commitment.

TextENGNew: ``...'Everything in your power'?@k@vS03/01/140100375. Please stop being vague.@k@vS03/01/140100376. 'Everything in your power' isn't enough -- it has to be certain!!``
changeReason: Changes Keiichi's response to match the teacher's different phrasing; alters the specific demand being made.

TextENGNew: ``If they decide to just wait and see again... how will you take responsibility?!``
The phone rang while I was still yelling.
Sensei snatched up the receiver immediately.

``...Hello!@k@vS03/22/141400055. Oh... my apologies for interrupting your training.@k@vS03/22/141400056. ...Yes.@k@vS03/22/141400057. It's regarding the situation with Satoko Houjou.@k@vS03/22/141400058. ...Yes.``
Sensei waved us off, motioning that we could go.
...Mion started toward the door, but I held my ground.
...I had a duty to make sure she didn't say anything vague or wishy-washy.@k ...If it looked like she wasn't going to say what needed to be said, I would grab that phone and speak the truth myself.
``...Yes.@k@vS03/22/141400062. ...I haven't confirmed it yet, but... from what the students are saying, the situation is quite serious.``
``Do something about it!!@k@vS03/01/140100380. Right now!!@k@vS03/01/140100382. If they decide to just sit back and observe...@k@vS03/01/140100383. I will never, EVER forgive you!!!``
I shouted at both Sensei and the principal on the other end of the line.
...I wanted to convey, in any way I could... just how dire Satoko's situation truly was.
``I understand that!@k@vS03/22/141400064. I will make sure they know!@k@vS03/22/141400065. Class president, please take Maebara-kun back to the classroom.``
changeReason: Changes form of address from 'Mion-kun' to 'Class president' (委員長).

TextENGNew: ``Oh... yes, ma'am.``
``...Come on, Kei-chan.@k@vS03/03/140300156. Let's leave the rest to the teachers.``
``.........You'd better... keep your promise...``
Until the very last moment... I fixed Sensei with my glare.
...Chie-sensei was actually wilting under my gaze.@k ...No, don't falter here. This isn't the time for that.@k ...This is where you give me a firm nod and tell me it's going to be all right...!
But... we'd already handed things over to her.
...From here... all we could truly do was pray.
Click.@k ...Mion shut the teachers' lounge door behind us.
changeReason: Changes sound effect from 'Bang' to 'Click' (ピシャリ = sharp shutting sound).
